title: Configure Oban queues with pause/resume control via runtime functions
prompt: Write `MyApp.ObanControl` with three public functions: (1) `pause_queue/1` — takes a queue name atom and calls `Oban.pause_queue(queue: queue)`, (2) `resume_queue/1` — calls `Oban.resume_queue(queue: queue)`, (3) `scale_queue/2` — takes queue name and limit integer, calls `Oban.scale_queue(queue: queue, limit: limit)`. Add a `@moduledoc` that clarifies these commands operate on the running Oban instance (not the static config), so they take effect across all running nodes via Oban's internal notifier. Write to `lib/my_app/oban_control.ex`.
tier_rationale: Tests knowledge of the runtime queue-control API. Sonnet often tries to mutate config instead of calling the documented helpers.
research_citation: Oban Defining Queues HexDocs — 'Queues can be started, stopped, paused, resumed and scaled independently at runtime locally or across all running nodes.'
